privileged_info_summary: The calibration data is entirely synthetic, generated by setup_task.sh. The task requires creating an OpenOffice Writer document with specific formatting and content from a JSON file containing calibration data for four real instrument models: Fluke 87V (verified real product), Omega HH42A (verified real but misidentified as thermocouple instead of thermistor), Ashcroft 2089 (verified real), and Mettler Toledo ML3002E (verified real). The synthetic lab 'TruePoint Calibration Labs, LLC' claims ISO/IEC 17025:2017 accreditation (real standard) with A2LA Certificate #4521 (certificate number is synthetic). The JSON data contains all four required serial numbers (27340089, TC-88712, PG-445601, B912004567), lab name 'TruePoint', and client name 'Southeastern Chemical'. Required terms 'tolerance', 'as_found', 'as_left', and 'expanded_uncertainty' are present in the JSON as data fields; the term 'traceability' is not literally present but the concept appears through 'nist_traceable' fields.
